message:
feat(core,parser,planner,vdbe): normalize "main" schema + comprehensive schema-qualified TableStar coverage

Polish the schema-qualified TableStar work with a canonical
treatment of the implicit "main" schema name and a sweep of
follow-on test coverage across every layer.  Before this commit,
`SELECT main.t.*` against a bare-schema table `t` would fail
because the planner treated "main" as an explicit schema
qualifier and never matched the bare-schema table source.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs

- New helper normalized_attached_schema_name(Option<&str>) ->
  Option<&str>:
    Some("main") (case-insensitive) → None
    anything else → passthrough
  Centralizes the "main is the implicit schema" rule so every
  matcher in the file routes through it.

- qualified_name_matches_table_binding() rewritten:
    When the target TableStar has no explicit schema, match
    by alias or bare name as before.
    When the target has an explicit schema, normalize both
    sides via normalized_attached_schema_name and compare the
    resulting Option<&str> values, then match on the bare
    table name.  This lets `main.t.*` match a table in the
    implicit main schema, `aux.t.*` match a table in the aux
    schema, and `aux.t.*` against a bare `t` correctly return
    false.

- New regression test
  test_select_explicit_main_table_star_from_bare_main_table:
    1. Open in-memory connection with an ATTACHed aux DB.
    2. Create `t` (in main) and `aux.t` with the same columns.
    3. Insert a distinctive row in each ('main-row' vs
       'aux-row').
    4. Run `SELECT main.t.* FROM t` and assert only the
       'main-row' row is returned.
    Before the fix this test would fail because the planner
    didn't recognize 'main' as equivalent to the unqualified
    binding.

crates/fsqlite-parser/src/semantic.rs
crates/fsqlite-parser/src/semantic_test.rs

- Resolver::resolve_result_column, TableStar arm:
    Normalize the qualifier's schema via the helper before
    calling scope.has_alias(), so has_alias('main.t') resolves
    against a bare 't' binding.
- Extend the unresolved-table error to carry the canonical
  schema.table form (via QualifiedName::to_string) so users
  see the same string they typed, not a half-stripped form.

crates/fsqlite-planner/src/lib.rs

- resolve_single_table_result_columns_with_options()
  TableStar branch applies the same normalization:
    if qualifier_matches_table(&normalized_schema, table_name,
                               table_alias) { ... }
  This fixes `SELECT main.t.*` against a single-table plan
  where the planner was otherwise rejecting the qualifier.

- Cross-reference between the new helper in connection.rs
  and the planner's matcher — they use the same rule so a
  TableStar that the core accepts also plans cleanly.

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs

- Cursor-side TableStar resolution (inside the join codegen
  and single-table read path) gets the same "normalize main"
  treatment so runtime cursor routing matches the planner's
  decision.
- New tests exercise the end-to-end flow against both the
  in-memory test harness and the compat oracle: build a
  two-schema setup, SELECT main.t.* and aux.t.* in separate
  queries, and assert each returns only the rows from the
  matching schema.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
